headline: 'Walmart's a canary in the coal mine' as inflation tests consumers
summary: Portfolio Wealth Advisors President and CIO Lee Munson joins Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi to discuss the threat of "peak tolerance" for inflation, warning that while higher-income individuals are still spending, a broader consumer pullback could emerge by fall as interest rates and high prices begin to crimp the market. Sozzi takes a closer look at Walmart (WMT) using Yahoo Finance's new AlphaSpace, a premium investment platform that combines data, charting, news, analysis, and more to help retail investors understand markets.

content.title: 'Walmart's a canary in the coal mine' as inflation tests consumers
content.description:
Portfolio Wealth Advisors President and CIO Lee Munson joins Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi to discuss the threat of "peak tolerance" for inflation, warning that while higher-income individuals are still spending, a broader consumer pullback could emerge by fall as interest rates and high prices begin to crimp the market.

Sozzi takes a closer look at Walmart (WMT) using Yahoo Finance's new AlphaSpace, a premium investment platform that combines data, charting, news, analysis, and more to help retail investors understand markets.

headline: Oil prices are hard-pressing consumers at bottom of K-shaped economy
summary: Walmart (WMT) came out with first quarter results that were in line with earnings and revenue estimates, with executives citing headwinds tied to consumers under additional price stresses. Gradient Investments senior portfolio manager Keith Gangl discusses the impact that higher gas prices (RB=F) are having on consumer sentiment and Walmart's customer base. Catch Yahoo Finance's interview with Walmart CFO John David Rainey.

content.title: Oil prices are hard-pressing consumers at bottom of K-shaped economy
content.description:
Walmart (WMT) came out with first quarter results that were in line with earnings and revenue estimates, with executives citing headwinds tied to consumers under additional price stresses.

Gradient Investments senior portfolio manager Keith Gangl discusses the impact that higher gas prices (RB=F) are having on consumer sentiment and Walmart's customer base.

Catch Yahoo Finance's interview with Walmart CFO John David Rainey.

headline: Why Walmart stock sold off after earnings and should you buy the dip?
summary: Investig.com -- Walmart shares fell after the retail giant posted first-quarter results that largely met expectations but failed to deliver the kind of upside investors had grown accustomed to over the past two years. Despite the muted reaction, analysts at D.A. Davidson reiterated a “Buy” rating and maintained a $150 price target, arguing that Walmart’s long-term growth drivers remain firmly intact.

content.title: Why Walmart stock sold off after earnings and should you buy the dip?
content.summary: Investig.com -- Walmart shares fell after the retail giant posted first-quarter results that largely met expectations but failed to deliver the kind of upside investors had grown accustomed to over the past two years. Despite the muted reaction, analysts at D.A. Davidson reiterated a “Buy” rating and maintained a $150 price target, arguing that Walmart’s long-term growth drivers remain firmly intact.

headline: Strong Retail Earnings in a Tough Environment
summary: Strong results from Walmart's general merchandise business is a key takeaway from its results. The very strong sales performance at Target confirms this favorable reading, suggesting that the persistent softness in discretionary spending categories in the post-COVID period may have run its course.

content.title: Strong Retail Earnings in a Tough Environment
content.summary: Strong results from Walmart's general merchandise business is a key takeaway from its results. The very strong sales performance at Target confirms this favorable reading, suggesting that the persistent softness in discretionary spending categories in the post-COVID period may have run its course.
